title: Continue-as-new integration test with recovery
purpose: Prove end-to-end that a looping workflow can use continue-as-new to bound its history, and that recovery correctly replays only the current run. This is the integration-level verification that the entire AH cluster works together: the event, the status, the recorder, the engine method, list_active exclusion, and chain navigation.
task: Write an integration test (in crates/aion/tests/) that: (1) starts a workflow that calls continue-as-new after a few iterations; (2) verifies the old run is terminal with ContinuedAsNew status; (3) verifies the new run is active with the carried input; (4) simulates recovery by rebuilding the engine from the store and verifying only the new run is replayed; (5) verifies the continuation chain is navigable via read_run_chain. Use InMemoryStore for the test.

requirements.title: Integration test: continue-as-new lifecycle
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have an integration test that starts a workflow, triggers continue-as-new via Engine::continue_as_new, and verifies: the old run's status is ContinuedAsNew, the new run's status is Running, the new run's input matches the carried payload, and the new run's WorkflowStarted has parent_run_id matching the old run.
acceptance: Test starts a workflow and calls engine.continue_as_new with a carried payload.
Old run's projected status is ContinuedAsNew.
New run exists under the same WorkflowId with a different RunId.
New run's history starts with WorkflowStarted carrying the carried payload.
New run's WorkflowStarted has parent_run_id = old run's RunId.

requirements.title: Integration test: recovery skips old runs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have an integration test that verifies list_active returns only the current (non-ContinuedAsNew) run after a continue-as-new. This proves recovery would replay only the current run.
acceptance: After continue-as-new, list_active returns only the WorkflowId once (not twice).
The active run is the new run, not the old one.
A workflow that has not used continue-as-new still appears correctly in list_active.

requirements.title: Integration test: continuation chain navigation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have an integration test that calls continue-as-new twice and verifies read_run_chain returns three RunSummary entries in order with correct parent_run_id links.
acceptance: After two continue-as-new calls, read_run_chain returns 3 entries.
Second entry has parent_run_id = first RunId.
Third entry has parent_run_id = second RunId.

boundaries: SHALL NOT test SDK-level workflow.continue_as_new — that requires Gleam fixtures and belongs in a separate brief.
SHALL NOT test server API endpoints — those belong in the aion-server cluster.
SHALL use InMemoryStore for all tests (no external dependencies).
